spec_match.rationale: The page now models the draft response-header mechanism directly: Connection-Allowlist / Connection-Allowlist-Report-Only, response-origin patterns, redirects, WebRTC handling, and report-only disposition. It explicitly states there is no navigator.connectionAllowlist setter.

interactive.rationale: The demo has editable policy and request inputs, three spec-relevant selects, and preset buttons for report-only and empty-policy edge cases.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and controls align with the feature index by showing outbound endpoint allowlisting for document and worker-initiated requests.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces malformed URL handling, empty allowlists, report-only violations, redirect blocking, and WebRTC policy choices.

references.rationale: The references section links to chromestatus.com/feature/5175745573945344 and the WICG draft spec.

design_system.rationale: The edited page uses shared CSS variables and pairs button background overrides with explicit colors.

summary: Reworked as a header-policy inspector with live response-header generation, report-only/redirect/WebRTC controls, and no remaining major findings.
